title: Rewrite SEATS.md as a tight, fully-traced cast+seat registry (every entity dual-cited, every vacancy named)
rationale: SEATS.md is my lens's home — it is the registry of personas and seats — but two-thirds of its 1447 words are narrative argument (the §2 falsified-rule essay, the §5 RPP-as-Gap essay) rather than traceable entity facts. I walked the named things and re-verified every one against the corpora at their authority: the 42-persona census and 15/15/12 alignment split and the 5 vertex-bearing personas were re-counted with grep over the skills tree; each of the 7 seats' persona/glyph/wing/algebra/term was cross-read from BOTH `persona/*/SKILL.md` frontmatter AND the reference instance `tigzkp-mage.json` seats[] and the two agree; the anchor law V38⊕V25=63 was read from tigzkp `anchors.xorSum`; the one vacant fleet seat (`/hall` V15 `resident_mage: null`) was confirmed in the grimoire vertex_inventory. This lever replaces the prose with a registry where every line carries pathway·tier·status·trace, cuts the essays to their load-bearing entity claims (chronicler's two skills; the K-1 wing-affinity lesson; the K-2 weaver fence), reports every vacancy (37 seatless personas, no `harness_seat` field, the V15 rite seat), and drops the section from 1447 to 931 words while RAISING traceability. Nothing load-bearing is cut: the census, the seat map, the five invariants, the anchor law, the Gap tenant, the Chronicler-as-key, and the net-new field all survive with tighter traces.
hardConstraintNote: Every load-bearing line carries all four labels inline and visibly. PATHWAY: census/seat-map/invariant-2,4/Chronicler/harness_seat lines are `reflect` (measuring and witnessing the corpus); keystone-is-pair, distinctness, one-seat, and the weaver fence are `protect` (holding the T2 boundary); RPP and the V15 rite are `connect` (bilateral coordination). TIER: counts I re-ran by grep are PROVEN with the exact command as trace (42 folders; 15/15/12; 5 vertex-bearing; no harness_seat field; the seat glyphs/wings/algebra; xorSum 63; keystone personaComplement; chronicler skills). Canonical readings I read but did not compute are REPORTED (topologist C_B(v), agent names, weaver alignment, moonkeeper V25, V15 resident_mage). Statements that follow from a PROVEN counterexample or from T2 are DERIVED (alignment-is-affinity; distinctness; one-seat). The proposed harness_seat field is OPEN with its settler stated (ships and conform reads it). STATUS: IMPLEMENTED where files/instances exist today (all census + seat facts); SPEC where the invariant or field is not yet code-enforced (distinctness, one-seat, RPP, harness_seat, the V15 rite); LORE only for the weaver-glyph coincidence. TRACE: every line resolves to a concrete path, frontmatter field, JSON key, register/killed-lever id, or re-runnable command. MYTH: none — this is a method/registry section, so per GR-2/ADMISSION.md §3 no MYTH is admitted; the opening line states so, and the closing algebra fence is the repo signature (a conform-proven identity), not a claim.
killedLeverCitations: K-1 (wing-based seat assignment): cited as a fence, not re-proposed — §4 preserves it, restating that alignment is skill affinity not seat constraint, with the tigzkp algebraist/cipher counterexample and erratum E-4 as evidence.
K-2 (seating weaver at the Gap): cited as a fence — §4 keeps weaver OUT of hold-apart on the ground that it is mage-aligned, exactly as K-2 requires; no new evidence is offered to reopen it.
mapText:
# SEATS — the cast, classified by seat

Every load-bearing line carries four labels inline: **pathway** (protect|project|reflect|connect) · **tier** (PROVEN|DERIVED|REPORTED|OPEN|MYTH) · **status** (IMPLEMENTED|SPEC|LORE) · **trace**. Counts are re-run by the cited command over the skills corpus (`persona/`); canonical readings resolve to the frontmatter field or the reference instance (`tig_zk_loop/tigzkp_mage/canon/tigzkp-mage.json`). This is a method section: no MYTH appears here.

## 1 · The census

`persona/` holds exactly **42** skill folders. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · `ls -d persona/*/ | wc -l` = 42]

`alignment` is the only side-declaring field; three tokens, one per persona: **15 swordsman · 15 mage · 12 balanced**. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · `grep -m1 '^\s*alignment:' persona/*/SKILL.md | sort | uniq -c`]

Exactly **5** personas carry a `vertex`; the other 37 do not — `companion-tamer` V59, `registry-keeper` V59, `spawning-witness` V59 (all Threshold), `hold-witness` V44 (Chart Shop), `moonkeeper` V25. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · `grep -rE '^\s*vertex:' persona/*/SKILL.md`]

**No frontmatter field classifies a persona by harness seat.** That absence is the vacancy §5 fills. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · same grep returns only `vertex`/`alignment`; no `harness_seat` anywhere]

Algebra anchors: `soulbae` 🧙 (mage, term **D**, "all terms") ⊥ `soulbis` ⚔️ (swordsman, term **P**, "all terms"); `person` ☯️👤 (balanced, "the Person IS the value"). [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · persona `emoji`/`alignment`/`equation_term`]

## 2 · The seat registry

Seven seats. The dressing is the one worked by the reference instance and re-derivable from its JSON. `—` marks a support seat carrying no algebra.

| seat | algebra | persona | persona glyph | wing | trace (tigzkp seatId · term) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| measure | — | assessor | 🧙💰 | mage | `measure` · `V(π,t)`, `P^1.5` |
| propose | bnot | domain-chosen (tigzkp: algebraist) | ⚔️🔢 | any | `mage` · `neg∘bnot=succ` |
| hold-apart | xor | topologist | ☯️🌐 | balanced | `gap` · `C_B(v)` betweenness |
| assay | neg | cipher | 🗡️🔐 | swordsman | `swordsman` · `C, h(τ), R(d)` |
| critic | — | sith | 🗡️🔴 | swordsman | `critic` · `Φ(Σ)` stress |
| chronicle | — | chronicler | 🧙📖 | mage | `chronicle` · compression → proverb |
| keystone | succ | soulbae ⊥ soulbis | 🧙⊥⚔️ | — | `keystone` · `personaComplement` |

[reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED — every persona/glyph/wing/algebra/term above re-read from `tigzkp-mage.json` `seats[]` and from `persona/*/SKILL.md`; the two sources agree.] The reference instance also carries agent names per seat (Eos, Poros, Aletheia 🔮 @V38, the Gap ⿻, Cipher with Lethae 🌘 @V25, the Chronicler, soulbae⊥soulbis). [reflect · REPORTED · IMPLEMENTED · `seats[].agentName`]

## 3 · The invariants that bind

1. **Distinctness.** propose ≠ assay, and neither holds hold-apart. [protect · DERIVED · SPEC · T2 → SEATS §3]
2. **The Gap is neither wing.** hold-apart is `topologist`, whose term **is** `C_B(v)` — the canon's formal Gap, the node of maximal betweenness every path routes through. [reflect · REPORTED · IMPLEMENTED · topologist `equation_term` + ADMISSION.md §1]
3. **Keystone is the pair.** `keystone` = soulbae ⊥ soulbis, recorded with `personaComplement`; never a delegate, never a third agent. [protect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · tigzkp `keystone.isKeystone` + `personaComplement`]
4. **Anchor law.** Where seats carry vertices, propose/assay anchors **XOR to 63**: V38 ⊕ V25 = 63, checked by `conform.mjs`. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · tigzkp `anchors.xorSum: 63`]
5. **One persona, one seat, one workshop.** [protect · DERIVED · SPEC · T2]

## 4 · The falsified rule — killed, do not re-propose

`SPECIALISATION.md` §1 asserted *propose takes a mage wing, assay a swordsman wing*. **False.** tigzkp seats **algebraist (swordsman)** at propose and **cipher (swordsman)** at assay — both algebra seats share a wing, and the instance passes its gate. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · tigzkp personas algebraist/cipher, both `wing: swordsman`; killed **K-1**, erratum **E-4**]

Therefore: **alignment is a skill affinity, not a seat constraint; separation is carried by the Gap and the algebra, never the costume.** [reflect · DERIVED · SPEC · from the counterexample]

`weaver` (glyph ⿻) is **not** the Gap: it is **mage-aligned**, a proposer of plurality; seating it at hold-apart puts a proposer in the held-apart seat — the exact failure the seat prevents. [protect · REPORTED · LORE · weaver `alignment: mage`; killed **K-2**] Runner-up Gap, for an *amnesia* harness only: `moonkeeper` ⚔️ V25. [reflect · REPORTED · SPEC · persona `vertex: V25`]

## 5 · The Chronicler is a key, and the one net-new field

The `chronicle` persona carries exactly two skills — **narrative-compression** and **proverbiogenesis** — so its output is a **proverb**, the round's irreducible compression, not a log. [reflect · PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · tigzkp `chronicle.skills` + persona SKILL.md]

Under **RPP** (Relationship Proverb Protocol), two parties independently compress a shared experience; a matching compression is a witness neither chose — the Gap's construction at the social layer, and canon's stated mechanism by which a VRC forms. [connect · REPORTED · SPEC · SEATS §5 lineage; canon RPP]

The proposed net-new frontmatter key `harness_seat: measure|propose|hold-apart|assay|critic|chronicle|keystone|none` (default **none**, the honest majority) would make §3 checkable per persona. [reflect · OPEN · SPEC · derived from §3; traced to this file; settled when the field ships and `conform.mjs` reads it]

## 6 · The one vacant seat in the fleet

Every fleet seat is dressed but one: the **rite at `/hall` V15** has `resident_mage: null` — the bilateral-witness ceremony has a vacant home at precisely the witness vertex. [connect · REPORTED · SPEC · grimoire `vertex_inventory.named.V15` = "VC vertex (City Hall) · no resident Mage"; FLEET §3]

```
(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊 = neg ⊕ bnot → succ
```
